title: Remove auto-provision user functionality — require explicit sign-up before access
body:
## Issue description

Currently, the application auto-provisions a `user_profiles` row for every authenticated user on their first API request. This is handled by `ensure-profile-middleware.ts`, which runs globally on all `/api/v1` routes after auth middleware. It performs an `INSERT … ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` into `user_profiles` using the identity from the JWT token.

This means any user who obtains a valid Entra ID / B2C token can automatically access the application without an explicit sign-up step. There is no gating mechanism to distinguish between "authenticated" and "registered/provisioned" users.

The desired behavior is that authentication alone should **not** grant application access. Users must explicitly sign up (register) to be provisioned in the system and allowed to use the application.

---

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

### 1. Remove the auto-provisioning middleware
- Remove or disable `createEnsureProfileMiddleware` from the global middleware chain in `src/app.ts` (currently registered at lines ~191-194).
- The middleware in `src/shared/auth/ensure-profile-middleware.ts` should be removed or archived.
- After removal, `GET /api/v1/me` must return **404** for authenticated users who have not explicitly signed up.

### 2. Create an explicit sign-up / registration endpoint
- Add a new endpoint (e.g., `POST /api/v1/signup` or `POST /api/v1/register`) that:
  - Accepts the authenticated user's identity from the JWT token.
  - Accepts required registration fields (at minimum `displayName`; optionally `avatarUrl`).
  - Creates the `user_profiles` row explicitly.
  - Returns the newly created profile.
- This endpoint should be the **only** path to provision a new user profile.
- The endpoint must be accessible to authenticated but not-yet-registered users.

### 3. Gate all protected API routes behind registration check
- After removing auto-provisioning, introduce a middleware or guard that checks whether the authenticated user has a `user_profiles` row.
- If the user is authenticated but **not** registered (no profile row), all protected endpoints (except the sign-up endpoint and `GET /api/v1/me`) should return an appropriate error response (e.g., `403 Forbidden` with a code like `NOT_REGISTERED` or `REGISTRATION_REQUIRED`).
- `GET /api/v1/me` should remain accessible to authenticated-but-unregistered users and return **404** to signal that sign-up is needed.
- The sign-up endpoint itself must be exempt from the registration check (but still require authentication).

### 4. Update the frontend to handle the registration flow
- When the frontend receives a 404 from `GET /api/v1/me` or a 403 `REGISTRATION_REQUIRED` from other endpoints, it should redirect to a sign-up page.
- The sign-up page should collect the required registration fields and call the sign-up endpoint.
- After successful sign-up, the user should be redirected to the main application.

### 5. Preserve foreign key integrity
- Eight tables have foreign key constraints referencing `user_profiles.id` (`groups`, `group_members`, `events`, `event_invitations`, `event_locations`, `event_chat_messages`, `event_checklist_items`, `event_drafts`).
- Without auto-provisioning, these FK constraints will cause failures if an unregistered user somehow reaches a write operation. The registration gate middleware (requirement 3) must prevent this.
- Ensure all write paths are protected so that only registered users can create groups, events, invitations, chat messages, checklist items, etc.

### 6. Update existing tests
- Remove or update tests that assert auto-provisioning behavior (e.g., the test in `profile.api.test.ts` that expects `GET /api/v1/me` to return 200 for a brand-new user).
- Update `ensure-profile-middleware.unit.test.ts` tests — either remove them or repurpose for the new registration-check middleware.
- Add new tests for the sign-up endpoint and registration-gate middleware.

---

## Additional notes

### Architecture alignment
- This change aligns with the project's **privacy-first** and **explicit access control** principles from `copilot-instructions.md`. Auto-provisioning implicitly grants access; explicit sign-up is a deliberate authorization step.
- The new sign-up endpoint should follow the existing modular architecture and live within the `identity-profile` module.
- Follow clean architecture: domain validation for registration, application use case for sign-up, infrastructure persistence, presentation router.

### Affected files (non-exhaustive)
- `src/shared/auth/ensure-profile-middleware.ts` — remove or repurpose
- `src/shared/auth/ensure-profile-middleware.unit.test.ts` — remove or repurpose
- `src/app.ts` — remove ensure-profile middleware registration; add registration-gate middleware
- `src/modules/identity-profile/presentation/profile.router.ts` — add sign-up endpoint
- `src/modules/identity-profile/presentation/profile.api.test.ts` — update auto-provision test
- `src/modules/identity-profile/infrastructure/knex-user-profile.repository.ts` — `ensureExists()` may be removed or replaced with an explicit `create()` method
- `client/` — add sign-up page, update auth flow to handle 404/403 for unregistered users

### Migration considerations
- Existing users already have `user_profiles` rows, so no data migration is needed.
- For any deployment, the registration-gate middleware must be deployed alongside the removal of auto-provisioning to avoid breaking existing users' flows.

### Security considerations
- The sign-up endpoint must still require a valid JWT token (authentication). It should not be publicly accessible.
- Rate limiting on the sign-up endpoint should be considered to prevent abuse.
- The registration-gate middleware must not leak information about whether a user exists in the system.

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `ensure-profile-middleware.ts` is removed or disabled and no longer auto-creates user profiles on authenticated requests.
- [ ] A new sign-up endpoint exists (e.g., `POST /api/v1/signup`) that explicitly creates a `user_profiles` row for an authenticated user.
- [ ] The sign-up endpoint validates required fields (at minimum `displayName`) and returns the created profile.
- [ ] The sign-up endpoint requires authentication but does not require prior registration.
- [ ] A registration-gate middleware or guard rejects requests from authenticated-but-unregistered users on all protected endpoints (except sign-up and `GET /api/v1/me`) with a clear error code (e.g., `403 REGISTRATION_REQUIRED`).
- [ ] `GET /api/v1/me` returns 404 for authenticated users who have not signed up.
- [ ] `GET /api/v1/me` returns 200 with profile data for registered users.
- [ ] All eight tables with FK constraints on `user_profiles.id` are protected — unregistered users cannot reach write operations.
- [ ] The frontend handles the unregistered state by redirecting to a sign-up page.
- [ ] The frontend sign-up page collects required fields and calls the sign-up endpoint.
- [ ] After successful sign-up, the frontend redirects to the main application.
- [ ] Existing auto-provisioning tests are removed or updated.
- [ ] New unit tests cover the sign-up use case, including validation of required fields.
- [ ] New unit tests cover the registration-gate middleware.
- [ ] New API tests verify 404 on `GET /api/v1/me` for unregistered users and 200 after sign-up.
- [ ] New API tests verify 403 `REGISTRATION_REQUIRED` on protected endpoints for unregistered users.
- [ ] Authorization and privacy tests confirm that unregistered users cannot access any group, event, or collaboration data.
- [ ] All existing tests pass after the changes.
- [ ] CI pipeline passes (lint, typecheck, unit tests, integration tests, API tests).
